title: [lichen_l2.c:1698-1703] rx_link_key not zeroed on non-auth error paths
description:
Gap: The rx_link_key local buffer is only zeroed on the success path and auth failure, not on all error paths.

What: Lines 1698-1703 copy link_key for use during RX. Lines 1746-1747 zero it on auth failure, and line 1774 zeros it on success. But if validation fails earlier (lines 1751-1757 oversized packet check), the function returns without zeroing rx_link_key.

Why it matters: The 16-byte key material remains on the stack after the function returns. While stack memory will be overwritten by subsequent calls, defense-in-depth suggests zeroing key material on ALL exit paths. This is inconsistent with other paths in the same function.
close_reason: Already fixed: secure_zero(rx_link_key) is now called on all exit paths - line 1819 (auth failure), line 1827 (oversized packet), and line 1847 (success). Fixed in commit a24db525 'fix(zephyr): address 115 exhaustive code review findings'.
